summary:
Most classical hormones rely on indirect methods such as bloodstream transport rather than direct entry into cells; however, thyroid hormones can pass through membranes directly because they are extremely fat-soluble. Glucagon may have a slight ability to influence intracellular processes but primarily operates using typical signaling pathway mechanisms involving receptors outside the cell instead of entering fully within them. The summary captures the essential distinction between how different types of hormones interact with target tissues based on both common patterns observed across many substances and unique characteristics exhibited by certain classes like thyroxine/thyroglobulin complexes versus others including peptide-derived signals represented here exemplified specifically by glucocorticoids/glucose-regulating agents generally speaking!

Glucagon may have a slight ability to influence intracellular processes but primarily operates using typical signaling pathway mechanisms involving receptors outside the cell instead of entering fully within them.
